prompt: Estimate the cost for ProjectNova, a greenfield cloud-native SaaS platform with no historical data available. Team of 8, 12-month timeline, microservices architecture.
assertions: recognizes absence of historical data limits analogous estimation accuracy
falls back to parametric and three-point techniques as primary methods
uses industry benchmarks (ISBSG, COCOMO II) as proxy when organizational data is absent
produces three-point estimates with O, M, P for each cost element
calculates confidence intervals at P50, P80, and P95
flags estimate as having higher uncertainty (>+/-35%) due to no analogues
tags all benchmark-derived values with [SUPUESTO] or [INFERENCIA]
output file named XX_cost_estimate_ProjectNova_{WIP}.md

prompt: Perform a parametric cost estimation using function points for ProjectERP, an SAP S/4HANA implementation with 1,200 identified function points, 3 integration interfaces, and a 15-person team across 2 geographies.
assertions: applies IFPUG or COSMIC function point costing model
incorporates cost-per-function-point rate with evidence source
adjusts for COCOMO II cost drivers: platform complexity, team distribution, integration difficulty
accounts for geographic labor rate differentials between the 2 locations
decomposes cost by phase (blueprint, realization, testing, go-live)
includes sensitivity analysis showing top parameters by cost impact
cross-validates with at least one secondary parametric model
regression R-squared or confidence band is stated for the chosen model

prompt: We have 3 estimates for ProjectBridge that diverge significantly: analogous says $2.1M, parametric says $3.4M, and three-point PERT says $2.8M. Reconcile these into a single defensible number.
assertions: calculates pairwise divergence percentages (analogous vs parametric = ~38%)
flags the analogous-parametric gap as exceeding 20% threshold
investigates root causes for each divergence (Data, Assumption, Model, or Scope)
does NOT simply average the three estimates
assigns credibility weights to each technique with documented rationale
produces a single reconciled estimate with confidence range
includes a decision log for every judgment call made during reconciliation
recommends contingency reserve based on remaining uncertainty

prompt: ProjectOmega had a baseline cost estimate of $1.5M but just received a 40% scope increase after Phase 2 approval. Re-estimate the project cost incorporating the scope change.
assertions: does NOT simply multiply baseline by 1.4 — analyzes the nature of added scope
decomposes the scope change into specific new work packages or deliverables
re-estimates affected cost elements using three-point technique for new scope
preserves original estimates for unchanged scope elements
recalculates contingency reserve reflecting increased uncertainty from mid-project change
flags non-linear cost impacts: rework, re-integration, extended coordination overhead
updates confidence intervals at P50, P80, P95 for the revised total
recommends change control board review before baselining the new estimate
